top_findings.title: ?author= filter is non-functional — uses a body extractor, not a query param
top_findings.evidence: src/lib.rs:131-134 get_books(State, author: Option<String>). In axum 0.7 `String`/`Option<String>` is a request-BODY extractor, not `Query<..>`; the `?author=` query string is never read. GET /books?author=X returns ALL books unfiltered. Confirmed by cargo build (compiles) + reading the handler.
top_findings.suggestion: Extract the filter via `Query(params): Query<HashMap<String,String>>` or a typed `Query<AuthorFilter>` and use params.get("author").

top_findings.title: Only 1 test present; task requires at least 3, and it exercises no endpoint
top_findings.evidence: src/lib.rs:328 only #[tokio::test] test_book_struct — a pure struct field-assignment test. tests/ directory is empty. `cargo test` = 1 passed. No handler/route/HTTP behavior is tested. Also violates the neutral prompt ('include tests that demonstrate the implementation meets the requirements').
top_findings.suggestion: Add integration tests using axum's `oneshot`/`tower::ServiceExt` to hit POST/GET/PUT/DELETE/health and the ?author= filter (>= 3 tests).

top_findings.title: PUT clobbers unspecified fields with defaults — partial update corrupts data
top_findings.evidence: src/lib.rs:232-235 uses payload.title.unwrap_or_default()/year.unwrap_or(0) etc., then UPDATE sets ALL columns. A partial PUT (e.g. the README:67-76 example with only title+year) overwrites author→"", isbn→"". UpdateBookRequest's all-Option fields imply partial update but the impl ignores that.
top_findings.suggestion: Fetch the existing row and only overwrite fields that are Some(..), or build a dynamic UPDATE for provided fields.
